user_outcome: sworn reads a real coach-produced status.json (object-form open_deferrals and verification.violations) without an unmarshal error and round-trips it without dropping any field, so the autonomous loop can run a real release instead of dying instantly on status-read.
rationale: D6 type drift is the single empirical blocker from the live fired dogfood run (docs/captures/2026-06-30-fired-dogfood-findings.md): sworn's Go carriers (state.Status.OpenDeferrals []string, state.Verification.Violations []string) lag the slice-status-v1 schema, which defines both as arrays of OBJECTS. fired's real open_deferrals are {id, description, why, tracking, acknowledged_by}. The fix migrates the Go types UP to the schema (D6 ratified direction) and round-trips the object form. No flatten-to-string read tolerance is permitted: sworn rewrites status.json every transition, so flattening would silently degrade real coach data on write-back. To bound churn and tech debt, display-only consumers read through string-projection helper methods rather than each being rewritten; only the type defs, the write path, the Rule-10 no-mock-boundary consumer, and the helpers change. Also reconciles need_ids->covers_needs (N-03) and adds the inconclusive leaf-enum (N-04 / #37). This is also the baton.Validate->ValidateSchema keystone prerequisite (step 1b). DESIGN-REVIEW AMENDMENT, REVISED (Pin 1, Coach-ratified 2026-07-01): real coach deferrals carry `acknowledged_by` but never the schema-required `acknowledgement` (127 such deferrals in the fired repo). The FIRST cut relaxed the schema to an anyOf over `acknowledgement` OR `acknowledged_by`; the Coach reversed that — an anyOf baked into the vendored schema is permanent drift from canonical baton, and `acknowledged_by` (who) is not the same field as `acknowledgement` (Rule 2's plain-text evidence the decision-maker was *told*). Since there is no wild data (all 127 deferrals are operator-owned), the canonical shape is defined STRICTLY and the data is migrated rather than the schema loosened. Canonical (ADDITIVE): open_deferrals item requires `why` + `tracking` + `acknowledgement` (the plain-text evidence) + `acknowledged_by` (structured attribution), with optional `acknowledged_at`; NO anyOf (AC-10). The 127 coach deferrals are migrated to add the plain-text `acknowledgement` (a cutover prerequisite before a strict-schema binary writes them — see AC-11). The canonical additive shape is pushed UP to baton (Rule 2 + slice-status-v1), so the vendored schema matches canonical with no drift — superseding #38's loosen-baton with improve-the-field.

effort_complexity.rationale: High complexity (Cynefin high): a careful type migration touching the Rule-10 no-mock-boundary consumer, with a round-trip-fidelity trap (schema requires `acknowledgement` but fired's real data uses `acknowledged_by`, so a naive typed struct silently drops fields). High effort: ~15 state-touching files across state, verify, run, implement, mcp, board, router, ledger, cmd. Atomic by nature (a type change cannot compile half-migrated), so it stays one slice despite sitting at the file-count ceiling.

acceptance_criteria.text: When state.Read parses a status.json whose open_deferrals items are JSON objects ({id, description, why, tracking, acknowledged_by}) and whose verification.violations items are JSON objects ({gate, description, evidence}), it SHALL unmarshal without error (no 'cannot unmarshal object into Go struct field ... of type string'). Proven by a fixture test using fired's real object shape.

When such a status.json is read via state.Read then written via state.Write, every field present on each original open_deferrals object and each violations object SHALL be preserved (no field dropped on write-back), proven by a round-trip fixture test asserting the re-marshalled objects retain id/description/acknowledged_by (fields not in the schema's required set).

state.Status.OpenDeferrals SHALL be typed []Deferral and state.Verification.Violations SHALL be typed []Violation, where Deferral and Violation are structs modelling the slice-status-v1 object shapes AND preserving unknown keys (matching the schema's additionalProperties:true), so no real-data field is lost in the Go representation.

Display-only consumers that previously read the []string deferral/violation values SHALL read through string-projection helper methods (e.g. Status.DeferralStrings(), Verification.ViolationStrings()) rather than each call site being rewritten, so the migration diff is bounded to the type definitions, the write path, the Rule-10 boundary consumer, the helpers, and the schema. Unrelated report types named Violations (ears, gate, designaudit, reqverify, specquality, lint, rtm) SHALL NOT be modified.

If CheckBoundaryMocks (internal/verify/verify.go) consumes deferrals to permit a declared mock at a validated boundary, it SHALL read the structured Deferral fields (item/why/tracking/acknowledgement) and its enforcement SHALL remain at least as strict as before; a regression test SHALL prove an undeclared mock at a validated boundary still fails closed (Rule 10).

The slice-status field carrying intake-need IDs SHALL serialise as covers_needs (state.Status field NeedIDs renamed CoversNeeds, json tag need_ids -> covers_needs), and its two writers (internal/implement/spec_record.go line ~58, cmd/sworn/task.go line ~145) SHALL be updated accordingly.

When the verifier returns an inconclusive verdict, the slice-status-v1 schema verification.result enum SHALL include 'inconclusive' so result: 'inconclusive' validates and persists; the merge gate's fail-closed treatment is unchanged (inconclusive still does NOT pass — it is merely representable rather than forced to 'fail'). (#37 / deferred D4).

AMENDED (Coach-ratified 2026-07-01, strict-direction / AC-11 sequencing): the original `sworn run` loop-run bar is un-runnable in-slice — AC-11 (ratified in the same 61df7ac replan) forbids running the fired loop on the strict-schema binary until the 127-deferral migration (#40) lands, and this slice DELIVERS that strict binary (AC-10), so the reachability bar is the READ path, not the mutating loop. When the strict-schema binary state.Read path (as exercised by `sworn board --release <R>` and a direct state.Read) is pointed at a live fired release whose status.json carries object-form open_deferrals ({id, why, tracking, acknowledged_by}) — the shape that previously crashed the D6 unmarshal — the read SHALL succeed with zero "cannot unmarshal object" errors, populating the named Deferral fields and preserving unknown keys in Extra. The full `sworn run` mutating loop on fired is explicitly deferred to the #40 cutover per AC-11 sequencing. Reachability artefact: the captured board render + direct-read output on a real fired object-form slice (e.g. 2026-06-16-critical-journey-resolutions / S05).
After the migration, `go build ./...` SHALL succeed and `go test ./...` SHALL pass (full suite, the regression boundary for ~15 touched files).

The slice-status-v1 open_deferrals item required-set SHALL be the STRICT ADDITIVE form [why, tracking, acknowledgement, acknowledged_by] with acknowledged_at optional, and SHALL NOT use an anyOf either-or. `acknowledgement` is Rule 2's plain-text evidence the decision-maker was told; `acknowledged_by` is the structured attribution (who); `acknowledged_at` is when. The Go Deferral struct SHALL carry acknowledgement, acknowledged_by and acknowledged_at and round-trip unknown keys (AC-02/AC-03 fidelity). When state.Write serialises a Status whose open_deferrals carry the full canonical shape, it SHALL validate and write without error; a deferral missing acknowledgement OR acknowledged_by SHALL fail closed. Proven by a canonical-shape round-trip fixture and a negative test. (The anyOf relaxation from the first cut is REMOVED; real coach data is migrated to the canonical shape, not tolerated — see AC-11.)

If a strict-schema binary (AC-10) is run against unmigrated coach data, validation fails closed — so before the strict schema reaches real data, the 127 operator-owned coach deferrals carrying `acknowledged_by` but no `acknowledgement` SHALL be migrated to add the Rule-2 plain-text `acknowledgement` (a one-time CUTOVER step on the coach data repo, NOT sworn production code in this slice), and the canonical additive shape SHALL be pushed UP to baton (Rule 2 + slice-status-v1) so the vendored schema matches canonical with no drift (supersedes #38). These are tracked cutover/upstream items; the slice's sworn code is AC-10. NB sequencing: do not run the fired loop on the strict-schema binary until the 127-deferral migration is applied.
